ocr.text: In Table 1, the comparison between Remifentanil/propofol (n = 39) and Midazolam/fentanyl (n = 33) across various parameters
reveals the following: for Material, the values are 11/10 (5–25) for Remifentanil/propofol and 11/11 (4–18) for
Midazolam/fentanyl with a P value of NS; Non-study drugs show 218/137 (31–1257) and 192/153 (55–723) respectively, also
with a P value of NS; for Study drugs, the values are 353/341 (54–835) for Remifentanil/propofol and 42/46 (19–63) for
Midazolam/fentanyl, with a significant P value of <0.05; Nurses' data indicates 869/827 (517–2213) versus 1,131/1,071 (554–
2,164), P <0.05; Physicians show 260/243 (135–723) compared to 352/301 (151–811), again with P <0.05; and Overall results
are 1,712/1,558 (1,058–3,647) for Remifentanil/propofol and 1,729/1,604 (821–3,337) for Midazolam/fentanyl, yielding a P
value of NS.
Remifentanil/propofol (n = 39)  Midazolam/fentanyl (n = 33)  P valuea
S.No  Emotional intelligence domain  Minimum score  Maximum score  Mean score  Standard deviation
1  Emotional Awareness (Max 40)  13  36  25.7391  4.7090
2  Emotional Management (Max 40)  0  38  25.1063  5.5755
3  Social Emotional Awareness (Max 40)  8  40  28.5700  5.2790
4  Relationship Management (Max 40)  8  40  28.1691  5.9834
5  Total Emotional Intelligence (Max 160)  55  148  107.58  16.4476
In Table 2, the Emotional Intelligence domains include Emotional Awareness with a Minimum score of 13, Maximum score of
36, Mean score of 25.7391, and Standard deviation of 4.7090; Emotional Management with Minimum 0, Maximum 38, Mean
25.1063, and Standard deviation 5.5755; Social Emotional Awareness with Minimum 8, Maximum 40, Mean 28.5700, and
Standard deviation 5.2790; Relationship Management with Minimum 8, Maximum 40, Mean 28.1691, and Standard deviation
5.9834; and Total Emotional Intelligence with Minimum 55, Maximum 148, Mean 107.58, and Standard deviation 16.4476.
